title: erythroxylum carthagenense
intro: Erythroxylum carthagenense, also known as the Carthaginian coca, is a plant species belonging to the Erythroxylaceae family. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in South America, for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive aid effects. The health benefits of Erythroxylum carthagenense include the treatment of fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anesthetic. The plant's leaves can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil for herbal preparations, although its use may vary depending on the region and cultural context.
